verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Much people of the Jews therefore knew that He was there, and they came, not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom He had raised from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As wealth is wont to hurl into destruction those who are not heedful, so also is power; the first leads into covetousness, the second into pride.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See, for instance, how the subject multitude of the Jews is sound, and their rulers corrupt; for that the first of these believed Christ, the Evangelists continually assert, saying, that “many of the multitude believed on Him” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 31, 48 ); but they who were of the rulers, believed not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they themselves say, not the multitude, “Hath any of the rulers believed on Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what saith one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The multitude who know not God are accursed” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 49 ); the believers they call accursed, and themselves the slayers, wise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this place also, having beheld the miracle, the many believed; but the rulers were not contented with their own evil deeds, they also attempted to kill Lazarus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Suppose they did attempt to slay Christ because He broke the Sabbath, because He made Himself equal to the Father, and because of the Romans whom ye allege, yet what charge had they against Lazarus, that they sought to kill him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is the having received a benefit a crime?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how murderous is their will?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He had worked many miracles; but none exasperated them so much as this one, not the paralytic, not the blind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this was more wonderful in its nature, and was wrought after many others, and it was a strange thing to see one, who had been dead four days, walking and speaking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: An honorable action, in truth, for the feast, to mix up the solemn assembly with murders.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, in the one case they thought to charge Him concerning the Sabbath, and so to draw away the multitudes; but here, since they had no fault to find with Him, they make the attempt on the man who had been healed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For here they could not even say that He was opposed to the Father, since the prayer stopped their mouths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then the charge which they continually brought against Him was removed, and the miracle was evident, they hasten to murder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that they would have done the same in the case of the blind man, had it not been in their power to find fault respecting the Sabbath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, that man was of no note, and they cast him out of the temple; but Lazarus was a person of distinction, as is clear, since many came to comfort his sisters; and the miracle was done in the sight of all, and most marvelously.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account all ran to see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then stung them, that while the feast was going on, all should leave it and go to Bethany.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They set their hand therefore to kill him, and thought they were not daring anything, so murderous were they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account the Law at its commencement opens with this, “Thou shalt not kill” ( Ex. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ); and the Prophet brings this charge against them, “Their hands are full of blood.” ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how, after not walking openly in Jewry, and retiring into the wilderness, doth He again enter openly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having quenched their anger by retiring, He cometh to them when they were stilled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, the multitude which went before and which followed after was sufficient to cast them into an agony; for no sign so much attracted the people as that of Lazarus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And another Evangelist saith, that they strewed their garments under His feet ( Matt. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8 ), and that “the whole city was moved” ( Matt. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ); with so great honor did He enter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He did, figuring one prophecy and fulfilling another; and the same act was the beginning of the one and the end of the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the, “Rejoice, for thy King cometh unto thee meek” ( Zech. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ), belonged to Him as fulfilling a prophecy, but the sitting upon an ass was the act of one prefiguring a future event, that He was about to have the impure race of the Gentiles subject to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how say the others, that He sent disciples, and said, “Loose the ass and the colt” ( Matt. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ), while John saith nothing of the kind, but that “having found a young ass, He sat upon it”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it is likely that both circumstances took place, and that He after the ass was loosed, while the disciples were bringing it, found (the colt), and sat upon it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they took the small branches of palm trees and olives, and strewed their garments in the way, showing that they now had a higher opinion concerning Him than of a Prophet, and said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Hosannah, blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that this most choked them, the persuasion which all men had that He was not an enemy of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this most divided the people, His saying that He came from the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what meaneth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Rejoice greatly, daughter of Zion”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because all their kings had for the most part been an unjust and covetous kind of men, and had given them over to their enemies, and had perverted the people, and made them subject to their foes; “Be of good courage,” It saith, “this is not such an one, but meek and gentle”; as is shown by the ass, for He entered not with an army in His train, but having an ass alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But this,” saith the Evangelist, “the disciples knew not, that it was written of Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that they were ignorant on most points, because He did not reveal to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He said, “Destroy this Temple, and in three days I will raise it up” ( c. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), neither then did the disciples understand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And another Evangelist saith, that “the saying was hid from them” ( Luke xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 34 ), and they knew not that He should rise from the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now this was with reason concealed from them, (wherefore another Evangelist saith, that as they heard it from time to time, they grieved and were dejected, and this because they understood not the saying concerning the Resurrection,) it was with reason concealed, as being too high for them: but why was not the matter of the ass revealed to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because this was a great thing also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe the wisdom of the Evangelist, how he is not ashamed to parade their former ignorance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That it was written they knew, that it was written of Him they knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it would have offended them if He being a King were about to suffer such things, and be so betrayed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, they could not at once have taken in the knowledge of the Kingdom of which He spake; for another Evangelist saith, that they thought the words were spoken of a kingdom of this world. ( Matt. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But the multitude bare witness that He had raised Lazarus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so many would not have been suddenly changed, unless they had believed in the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now this seems to me to be said by those who felt rightly, but had not courage to speak boldly, and who then would restrain the others by pointing to the result, as though they were attempting impossibilities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again they call the multitude “the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Scripture is wont to call by the name “world” both the creation, and those who live in wickedness; the one, when It saith, “Who bringeth out His world by number” ( Isa. xl.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26 ); the other when It saith, “The world hateth not you, but Me it hateth.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And these things it is necessary to know exactly, that we may not through the signification of words afford a handle to the heretics.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And there were certain of the Greeks that came up to worship at the Feast.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Being now near to become proselytes, they were at the Feast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore the report concerning Him was imparted to them, they say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We would see Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Philip gives place to Andrew as being before him, and communicates the matter to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But neither doth he at once act with authority; for he had heard that saying, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ): therefore having communicated with the disciple, he refers the matter to his Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they both spoke to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The hour is come, that the Son of Man should be glorified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is, “The hour is come”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He had said, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles,” (thus cutting away all excuse of ignorance from the Jews,) and had restrained the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore the Jews continued disobedient, and the others desired to come to Him, “Now,” saith He, “it is time to proceed to My Passion, since all things are fulfilled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if we were to continue to wait for those who are disobedient and not admit these who even desire to come, this would be unbefitting our tender care.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then He was about to allow the disciples to go to the Gentiles after the Crucifixion, and beheld them springing on before, He said, “It is time to proceed to the Cross.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He would not allow them to go sooner, that it might be for a testimony unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Until that by their deeds the Jews rejected Him, until they crucified Him, He said not, “Go and make disciples of all nations” ( Matt. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), but, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), and, “I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel” ( Matt xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ), and, “It is not meet to take the children’s bread and give it unto dogs.” ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when they hated Him, and so hated as to kill Him, it was superfluous to persevere while they repulsed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they refused Him, saying, “We have no king but Cæsar.” ( c. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that at length He left them, when they had left Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “How often would I have gathered your children together, and ye would not?” ( Matt. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is, “Except a grain of corn fall into the ground and die”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh of the Cross, for that they might not be confounded at seeing, that just when Greeks also came to Him, then He was slain, He saith to them, “This very thing specially causeth them to come, and shall increase the preaching of Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then since He could not so well persuade them by words, He goeth about to prove this from actual experience, telling them that this is the case with corn; it beareth the more fruit when it hath died.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now,” saith He, “if this be the case with seeds, much more with Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the disciples understood not what was spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the Evangelist continually putteth this, as making excuse for their flight afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This same argument Paul also hath raised when speaking of the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What sort of excuse then will they have who disbelieve the Resurrection, when the action is practiced each day, in seeds, in plants, and in the case of our own generation? for first it is necessary that the seed die, and that then the generation take place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, in short, when God doeth anything, reasonings are of no use; for how did He make us out of those things that were not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This I say to Christians, who assert that they believe the Scriptures; but I shall also say something else drawn from human reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of men some live in vice, others in virtue; and of those who live in vice, many have attained to extreme old age in prosperity, many of the virtuous after enduring the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then shall each receive his deserts?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At what season?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yea,” saith some one, “but there is no resurrection of the body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They hear not Paul, saying, “This corruptible must put on incorruption.” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaks not of the soul, for the soul is not corrupted; moreover, “resurrection” is said of that which fell, and that which fell was the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why wilt thou have it that there is no resurrection of the body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not possible with God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this it were utter folly to say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it unseemly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why is it unseemly, that the corruptible which shared the toil and death, should share also the crowns?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For were it unseemly, it would not have been created at the beginning, Christ would not have taken the flesh again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But to show that He took it again and raised it up, hear what He saith: “Reach hither thy fingers” ( c. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ); and, “Behold, a spirit hath not bones and sinews.” ( Luke xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did He raise Lazarus again, if it would have been better to rise without a body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why doth He this, classing it as a miracle and a benefit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did He give nourishment at all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Be not therefore deceived by the heretics, beloved: for there is a Resurrection and there is a Judgment, but they deny these things, who desire not to give account of their actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this Resurrection must be such as was that of Christ, for He was the first fruits, the first born of the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if the Resurrection is this, a purifying of the soul, a deliverance from sin, and if Christ sinned not, how did He rise again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how have we been delivered from the curse, if so be that He also sinned?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And now saith He, “The prince of this world cometh, and had nothing in Me”? ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They are the words of One declaring His sinlessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: According to them therefore He either did not rise again; or that He might rise, He sinned before His Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He both rose again, and did no sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He rose in the Body, and these wicked doctrines are nothing else than the offspring of vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then fly this malady.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, It is saith, “evil communications corrupt good manners.” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These are not the doctrines of the Apostles; Marcion and Valentius have newly invented them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then flee them, beloved, for a pure life profits nothing when doctrines are corrupt; as on the other hand neither do sound doctrines, if the life be corrupt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The heathen were the parents of these notions, and those heretics reared them, having received them from Gentile philosophers, asserting that matter is uncreated, and many such like things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then they asserted that there could be no Artificer unless there were some uncreated subject matter, so also they disallowed the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us not heed them, as knowing that the power of God is all sufficient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not heed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To you I say this; for we will not decline the battle with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the man who is unarmed and naked, though he fall among the weak, though he be the stronger, will easily be vanquished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had you given heed to the Scriptures, had you sharpened yourselves each day, I would not have advised you to flee the combat with them, but would have counseled you to grapple with them; for strong is truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since you know not how to use the Scriptures, I fear the struggle, lest they take you unarmed and cast you down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there is nothing, there is nothing weaker than those who are bereft of the aid of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If these heretics employ the wisdom of the Gentiles, we must not admire, but laugh at them, because they employ foolish teachers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those men were not able to find out anything sound, either concerning God or the creation, and things which the widow among us is acquainted with, Pythagoras did not yet know, but said that the soul becomes a bush, or a fish, or a dog.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To these, tell me, ought you to give heed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how could it be reasonable to do so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They are great men in their district, grow beautiful curls, and are enfolded in cloaks; thus far goes their philosophy; but if you look within there is dust and ashes and nothing sound, but “their throat is an open sepulcher” ( Ps. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ), having all things full of impurity and corruption, and all their doctrines (full) of worms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance, the first of them said that water was God, his successor fire, another one air, and they descended to things corporeal; ought we then, tell me, to admire these, who never even had the thought of the incorporeal God? and if they did ever gain it afterwards, it was after conversing in Egypt with our people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, that we bring not upon you much confusion, let us here close our discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For should we begin to set before you their doctrine, and what they have said about God, what about matter, what about the soul, what about the body, much ridicule will follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they will not even require to be accused by us, for they have attacked each other; and he who wrote against us the book concerning matter, made away with himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore that we may not vainly delay you, nor wind together a labyrinth of words, leaving these things we will bid you keep fast hold of the listening to the Holy Scriptures, and not fight with words to no purpose; as also Paul exhorteth Timothy ( 2 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14 ), filled though he was with much wisdom, and possessing the power of miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us now obey him, and leaving trifling; let us hold fast to real works, I mean to brotherly-kindness and hospitality; and let us make much account of alms-giving, that we may obtain the promised good things, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for endless ages.
